---
title: "WUR_Tutorial"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
BiocManager::install("microbiome/mia")

library(remotes)
install_github("microbiome/OMA", dependencies = TRUE, upgrade = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(mia)
data("GlobalPatterns", package = "mia")
tse <- GlobalPatterns
tse
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
tse <- tse[1:5, c(1, 19, 16)]
tse

```

```{r}
assays(tse)
assay(tse, "counts") |> head()
```

```{r}
mat <- matrix(nrow = nrow(tse), ncol = ncol(tse))
assay(tse, "empty_table", withDimnames = FALSE) <- mat
assays(tse)

assay(tse, "empty_table") |> head()

colData(tse)
rowData(tse)
```

```{r}

biom_file_path <- ("~/Downloads/Aggregated_humanization2.biom")
sample_meta_file_path <- ("~/Downloads/Mapping_file_ADHD_aggregated.csv")
tree_file_path <- ("~/Downloads/Data_humanization_phylo_aggregation.tre")

library(mia)
tse <- importBIOM(
    biom_file_path,
    rank.from.prefix = TRUE,
    prefix.rm = TRUE,
    artifact.rm = TRUE
)

tse

```

```{r}
# The imported BIOM did not contain any colData
# lets add colData (csv file with colnames in the first row and row nammes in the first collumn)
colData(tse)

# Check if that sample exists
if (is.null(rownames(sample_meta)) || is.null(colnames(tse))) {
  stop("Samples names missing", call. = FALSE)
}

# Sort rows in sample metadata to match with TreeSE
sample_order <- match(colnames(tse), rownames(sample_meta))
sample_meta <- sample_meta[sample_order, , drop = FALSE]

# if sample metadata DID NOT include all samples, missing ones are now names NA.
# That is why we replace sample metadata names with the ones in TreeSE

rownames(sample_meta) <- colnames(tse)

sample_order
colnames(tse)
sample_meta
```

```{r}
colData(tse)

colData(tse) <- DataFrame(sample_meta)
colData(tse) |> head()


```

```{r}
# Finally we add the phylogenetic tree to the rowData slot. 
# Such feature is available ONLY in TreeSE objects. Tree specifying the sample hierarchy can be stored in the colTree slot

tree <- ape::read.tree(tree_file_path)

rowTree(tse) <- tree

tse
rowData(tse)
rowTree(tse) |> head()
```

```{r}
#CSV import
count_file <- ("~/Downloads/taxonomy_abundance.csv")
tax_file<- ("~/Downloads/taxonomy_table.csv")
sample_file <- ("~/Downloads/sample_metadata.csv")

counts <- read.csv(count_file, row.names = 1) # Abundance table (e.g. ASV data; to assay data)
tax <- read.csv(tax_file, row.names = 1) # Taxonomy table (to rowData)
samples <- read.csv(sample_file, row.names = 1) # Sample data (to colData)

# Match rows and columns
counts <- counts[rownames(tax), rownames(samples)]

# Let's ensure that the data is in correct (numeric matrix) format:
counts <- as.matrix(counts)

# coldata rownames match assay colnames
all(rownames(samples) == colnames(counts)) # our dataset

class(samples)

# rowdata rownames match assay rownames
all(rownames(tax) == rownames(counts))
class(tax)

class(counts)

# Ensure that colnames of assay match with rownames of colData, and rownames of assay match with rownames of rowData.

# If your data do not have names, you have to be especially careful, since this can lead to errors!


 

```

```{r}
# Create a TSE

tse_taxa <- TreeSummarizedExperiment(
  assays = SimpleList(counts = counts),
  colData = DataFrame(samples),
  rowData = DataFrame(tax)
)

tse_taxa

# IMPORTANT!! 
# When creating TreeSE, assay must be a matrix, and both colData and rowData must be DataFrame objects.
```

```{r}
# Data resources 
data(package = "mia")

data("GlobalPatterns", package = "mia")
GlobalPatterns
```

```{r}
library(microbiomeDataSets)
availableDataSets()


data(HintikkaXOData, package = "mia")

mae <- HintikkaXOData
mae

```

```{r}
# Exercise 
library(mia)


### Defining FILE PATHS ###

url_to_assay <- url("https://github.com/microbiome/data/raw/main/OKeefeDSData/okeefe_assay.csv")
url_to_coldata <- url("https://github.com/microbiome/data/raw/main/OKeefeDSData/okeefe_coldata.csv")
url_to_rowdata <- url("https://github.com/microbiome/data/raw/main/OKeefeDSData/okeefe_rowdata.csv")


### Importing FILES ###

okeefe_assay <- read.csv(url_to_assay, row.names = 1)
colnames(okeefe_assay) <- gsub("\\.", "-", colnames(okeefe_assay))

okeefe_coldata <- read.csv(url_to_coldata, row.names = 1)
okeefe_rowdata <- read.csv(url_to_rowdata, row.names = 1)

okeefe_assay <- as.matrix(okeefe_assay)
### Constructing TSE ###

tse <- TreeSummarizedExperiment(assays = list(counts = okeefe_assay),
                                colData = okeefe_coldata,
                                rowData = okeefe_rowdata)


tse
class(okeefe_assay)

okeefe_rowdata<- DataFrame(okeefe_rowdata)
okeefe_coldata <- DataFrame(okeefe_coldata)
class(okeefe_coldata)
class(okeefe_rowdata)
### SAVE into RDS ###
saveRDS(tse, file = "tse.rds")


```
